rag_rules: Place AGREE contracts in component type scopes for port-level assumptions and guarantees, and in implementation scopes for subcomponent bindings.
Use annex agree {** ... **}; blocks for AGREE contract syntax and ensure all identifiers are visible in the current scope.
generic_rules: Preserve AADL scope and type consistency when generating AGREE contracts.
Use only visible AADL features or declared helpers in AGREE annexes.
Avoid introducing package-qualified identifiers not present in the model.
Ensure AGREE scalar types match AADL conventions (int, real, bool).
syntax_or_pattern_reminders: AGREE contracts use `assume`, `guarantee`, `eq`, `const`, and `assign` within `annex agree {** ... **};` blocks.
Stateful equations should use initialized temporal recurrence; branching logic uses nested `if ... then ... else`.
anti_patterns: Do not reference AADL property names (e.g., Deadline, Period) as AGREE variables.
Do not invent new identifiers or types not visible in the current AADL scope.
irrelevant_topics: AGREE validator diagnostics and repair patterns
